rules.title: Non-SDK-style project file
rules.remediation: Convert the project to the SDK style (<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">). Replace TargetFrameworkVersion with a TargetFramework moniker, move packages.config entries to PackageReference, and let the SDK glob source files instead of listing them. Do this before other migration work: nearly every later step assumes an SDK-style project.

rules.title: packages.config instead of PackageReference
rules.remediation: Migrate packages.config to PackageReference (Visual Studio offers an in-place migration, or run 'dotnet migrate'-style tooling). PackageReference is required for SDK-style projects and gives transitive restore.

rules.title: GAC reference (no HintPath)
rules.remediation: Replace the GAC reference with a NuGet package or an explicit HintPath to a checked-in assembly. The Global Assembly Cache does not exist on modern .NET, so GAC-resolved dependencies will not load.

rules.title: Package has no version supporting the target framework
rules.remediation: Replace the package with a version or successor that targets modern .NET, or remove the dependency. See the suggested replacement in the finding message.

rules.title: Package marked deprecated on nuget.org
rules.remediation: The package is deprecated by its maintainers. Move to the suggested replacement (named in the finding when nuget.org provides one) or an actively-maintained alternative. Deprecated packages stop receiving fixes and are a migration risk even when they still run.

rules.title: ASP.NET WebForms
rules.remediation: WebForms has no counterpart on modern .NET. Plan a re-architecture to Razor Pages, MVC, or Blazor. This is an architectural decision, not a mechanical port.

rules.title: System.Web dependency outside WebForms
rules.remediation: System.Web is not available on modern .NET. Replace the dependent code: HttpContext usage moves to Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http, HttpUtility to System.Web.HttpUtility's modern equivalents or System.Net.WebUtility.

rules.title: ConfigurationManager.AppSettings usage
rules.remediation: On modern .NET, either add the System.Configuration.ConfigurationManager package to keep reading app.config, or migrate to Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration (appsettings.json, environment variables, options pattern).

rules.title: WCF service host (server side)
rules.remediation: Server-side WCF (ServiceHost) is not part of modern .NET. Migrate the service to CoreWCF, or re-implement it as gRPC or a minimal/Web API. WCF client usage is separately supported via the System.ServiceModel.* client packages.

rules.title: .NET Remoting
rules.remediation: .NET Remoting was removed entirely and has no modern equivalent. Replace it with a supported transport: gRPC, WCF on CoreWCF, or an HTTP API. This needs an architectural decision.

rules.title: ASP.NET MVC 5 (System.Web.Mvc)
rules.remediation: ASP.NET MVC 5 (System.Web.Mvc) runs only on .NET Framework. Migrate to ASP.NET Core MVC (Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc). Controllers and views port with edits; routing, filters, and DI wiring change.

rules.title: System.Drawing.Common on non-Windows
rules.remediation: System.Drawing.Common is Windows-only on modern .NET and throws PlatformNotSupportedException elsewhere. If the app must run on Linux, switch to a cross-platform imaging library such as ImageSharp or SkiaSharp.

rules.title: Windows Registry access
rules.remediation: The Windows Registry does not exist on other platforms; Microsoft.Win32.Registry throws PlatformNotSupportedException there. Move configuration/state to a cross-platform store (config files, environment variables, a database).

rules.title: System.DirectoryServices / Active Directory
rules.remediation: System.DirectoryServices is Windows-only on modern .NET. For cross-platform directory access use System.DirectoryServices.Protocols (LDAP), which is supported on Linux, or an identity provider.

rules.title: Thread.Abort
rules.remediation: Thread.Abort is not supported on modern .NET and throws PlatformNotSupportedException. Replace it with cooperative cancellation using CancellationToken.

rules.title: BinaryFormatter
rules.remediation: BinaryFormatter is removed in .NET 9 and throws when invoked (it was also a well-known security risk). Replace it with a safe serializer: System.Text.Json, MessagePack, or protobuf. Changing serialization format may require a data migration.

rules.title: Code Access Security attributes
rules.remediation: Code Access Security is not honored on modern .NET. Remove CAS attributes (SecurityPermission, PermissionSet, PrincipalPermission) and enforce authorization at the application layer instead.

rules.title: System.Data.SqlClient
rules.remediation: System.Data.SqlClient is in maintenance mode. Switch to Microsoft.Data.SqlClient, and review the Encrypt=true default change in 4.0+ that can break existing connection strings (see MIG7002).

rules.title: Encoding.Default behavior change
rules.remediation: Encoding.Default is the system ANSI code page on .NET Framework but always UTF-8 on modern .NET. Make the intended encoding explicit (e.g. Encoding.UTF8 or a specific code page) wherever bytes cross a boundary.

rules.title: Code-page encoding without provider registration
rules.remediation: Modern .NET does not register legacy code-page encodings by default. Call Encoding.RegisterProvider(CodePagesEncodingProvider.Instance) at startup, or the Encoding.GetEncoding call throws for non-Unicode code pages.

rules.title: Target framework below 4.6.2
rules.remediation: Retarget the project to at least .NET Framework 4.6.2 before migrating. Versions below 4.6.2 lack full .NET Standard 2.0 support, which the migration path relies on. This is usually a low-risk retarget-and-rebuild step.

rules.title: ASMX web service
rules.remediation: ASMX web services have no counterpart on modern .NET. Re-implement the service as an ASP.NET Core Web API (or gRPC). Clients that consumed the ASMX endpoint need updating too.

rules.title: MSMQ (System.Messaging)
rules.remediation: System.Messaging (MSMQ) is not available on modern .NET. Move to a supported message queue such as Azure Service Bus or RabbitMQ, or a third-party MSMQ-compatible library.

rules.title: System.Management / WMI
rules.remediation: WMI (System.Management) is Windows-only on modern .NET and throws PlatformNotSupportedException elsewhere. If the app must run cross-platform, replace WMI queries with platform-appropriate APIs.

rules.title: EventLog
rules.remediation: System.Diagnostics.EventLog is Windows-only on modern .NET. Use a cross-platform logging framework (e.g. Microsoft.Extensions.Logging with a suitable sink) instead of writing to the Windows Event Log.

rules.title: Obsolete cryptography types
rules.remediation: The concrete *CryptoServiceProvider / *Managed types are obsolete on modern .NET. Create algorithms via base-class factories (SHA256.Create(), Aes.Create(), RandomNumberGenerator), and drop weak algorithms (MD5, SHA1, DES) entirely.

rules.title: System.Data.OleDb on non-Windows
rules.remediation: The OLE DB provider (System.Data.OleDb) is Windows-only on modern .NET. Use a native, cross-platform provider (e.g. Microsoft.Data.SqlClient, Npgsql) where possible.

rules.title: LINQ to SQL (System.Data.Linq)
rules.remediation: LINQ to SQL (System.Data.Linq) is not available on modern .NET. Migrate the data layer to Entity Framework Core or another supported ORM.
